steps.title: Find the pressure formula at the final depth
steps.content:
The diver starts at depth $h$ and descends $d$ additional meters, reaching a final depth of $h + d$ meters. Using the pressure formula at this new depth:

$$P_2 = 10(h + d) + 101 = 10h + 10d + 101$$

steps.title: Match with the given formula
steps.content:
We're told that $P_2 = 10d + 311$. Setting the two expressions equal:

$$10h + 10d + 101 = 10d + 311$$

Subtract $10d$ from both sides:

$$10h + 101 = 311$$

steps.title: Interpret the constant 311
steps.content: The expression $10h + 101$ is the original pressure formula $P = 10h + 101$ evaluated at the starting depth $h$. Since $10h + 101 = 311$, the constant 311 is the pressure the diver experienced at the original depth—right when the diver began to descend the additional $d$ meters. That matches choice D — D is correct.
